item.title: The Computer That Helped Win World War II
item.description: Colossus cracked Germany’s encrypted communications

item.title: What we know about the typical Polymarket user
item.description: The typical Polymarket user placed a total of 46 trades across 10 active trading days during our study period.

item.title: Planetary Intelligence, AI for the 21st Century Earth: Will Marshall's Case for a Smarter Planet
item.description: A new image of every point on the Earth’s landmass, captured once every 24 hours, with the ability to zoom in on specific locations at higher resolution.

item.title: What do people in middle-income countries think about cuts to U.S. humanitarian aid?
item.description: In 16 middle-income countries surveyed, many people are unsure how U.S. humanitarian aid cuts will affect them, though those with opinions lean negative.

item.title: U.S. foreign aid is plunging in second Trump administration
item.description: U.S. foreign aid fell to its lowest inflation-adjusted level in over two decades in fiscal 2025, dropping to $47.3 billion — more than $24 billion less than the prior year.

item.title: Why AI Needs a "Genie Coefficient"
item.description: Proposing a new metric for whether AI does what you actually want

item.title: IEEE Program Helps Girls In India See a Future In STEM
item.description: Hands-on projects and role models open doors for STEM careers

item.title: How global energy production is changing, in 6 charts
item.description: Fossil fuels still dominate global electricity, but wind and solar are surging. In 2025, renewables outpaced coal worldwide for the first time, supplying 34% of electricity.

item.title: We’re Squandering LEDs’ Potential to Save Our Night Skies
item.description: Bad decisions have turned efficient technology into a driver of light pollution. There is a better way

item.title: Ray Kurzweil at AI for Good Global Summit 2026: "We will not be replaced by AI — we will merge with it"
item.description: Connect with 55K+ AI enthusiasts

item.title: This Graduate Student Equips NASA’s Robots With Assembly Skills
item.description: Her algorithm enables robots to install antennas on satellites

item.title: Majorities of Americans say key financial milestones are harder for today’s young adults to reach
item.description: Most Americans say it’s harder for young adults to find a job, buy a house, pay for college and save for their future than it was for their parents' generation.

item.title: Mexicans view President Claudia Sheinbaum favorably, but less so than in 2025
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum remains popular, but her favorability has dropped to 64%. Views of the economy and democracy are also declining.

item.title: How we measured Americans’ engagement in public life
item.description: This piece explains how researchers measured Americans' engagement in public life using multimode surveys, address-based sampling and cluster analysis.

item.title: How Americans Are Engaged With News, Politics, Religion and Civic Life
item.description: Americans’ engagement with others is not one-dimensional. Instead, they sort into four distinct groups that reflect different patterns of participation in society.

item.title: Younger and older Americans engage in public life in different ways
item.description: Older adults are more likely to participate in civic activities like voting, donating or attending religious services, while younger adults engage more in online spaces.

item.title: Why Indonesia’s Fisheries Future Hinges On Data Integrity and Trust
item.description: From vessel tracking to algorithmic enforcement, a surveillance revolution is transforming marine governance

item.title: Stable Jobs Can Hide the Riskiest Move In Your Tech Career
item.description: Strive for better opportunities and cutting-edge skills

item.title: Notice to Membership
item.description: The IEEE Board of Directors sanctions a member

item.title: Inside ELIZA’s Source Code and Its Multiple Personalities
item.description: Uncovering ELIZA’s source code reveals hidden lessons

item.title: In conversation with Innovate Australia: Peter Kasprzak on supporting the AI for Good Innovation Factory Australia Local Chapter
item.description: The AI for Good Innovation Factory reaches its Grand Finale in Geneva each summer, following a year of activity across a large-scale international network. Regional chapters across multiple continents are hosted by organisations with deep knowledge of their local innovation ecosystems, helping to identify promising founders and select the strongest solutions to present before expert juries. With chapters already active in places including India, Catalonia and Malta, the network continues to expand, with Innovate Australia leading the initiative’s local chapter in Australia.

item.title: Turning Taboo Into Peace with Death: Let's Talk About It | IONS
item.description: Marilyn Schlitz presents a wealth of teachings and practical guidance on how to turn a taboo topic into a source of peace.

item.title: FIRST Global and Experiential Bring Agentic AI Learning Experience to 190+ Countries, Advancing Robotics Education
item.description: FIRST Global Joins the UN ITU AI Skills Coalition; FIRST Global and XRP Kits Offer New Agentic AI module powered by FYI.AI For Student Robotics Teams

item.title: Vietnam won two second prizes at the global finals of the Robotics for Good 2026 competition
item.description: According to information from the STEM Education Promotion Alliance (SEPA) on July 11th, the two Vietnamese teams excellently won second place in the

item.title: Day 4 at the AI for Good Global Summit: Women's leadership, education and human progress close the 2026 edition
item.description: GENEVA, July 10, 2026 - The AI for Good Global Summit concluded following four days of sessions that demonstrated the widespread application of artificial intelligence in a variety of sectors, including health and agriculture, mobility, education, creative industries, and public policy. The summit also raised awareness of quantum technologies, robotics, and the standards and infrastructure required to facilitate their widespread deployment.

item.title: AI for Good's Youth Zone Wraps Up With a Focus on Responsible Design
item.description: GENEVA, 10 July 2026 — Today marked the end of the programme at the AI for Good Global Summit 2026's Youth Zone, which revisited a number of sessions from earlier in the week, and introduced two new sessions on safety and creative problem-solving.

item.title: The Robotics for Good Youth Challenge 2026 comes to an end as winners are awarded
item.description: Connect with 55K+ AI enthusiasts

item.title: Top 3 things happening on Day 4 of the AI for Good Global Summit 2026
item.description: The Robotics for Good Youth Challenge Grand Finale will reach its conclusion on the Youth Stage from 14:10 to 14:45. After four days of competition, the challenge will come to an end, closing a season that opened the Youth Zone's programme on 7 July.

item.title: Fifteen minutes to convince the committee: meet the ITU Kaleidoscope 2026 winners
item.description: The 16th edition of the ITU Kaleidoscope academic conference wrapped up today in Geneva, after three days of peer-reviewed research presentations on AI and frontier technologies. The title of this conference, held under the auspices of the AI for Good Global Summit 2026, aptly reflects the nature of the entire programme: a weave of diverse perspectives emerging from the exchange among researchers from universities, industry and research institutions around the world to discuss how AI, quantum, space and strategic foresight can contribute to advancing sustainable and inclusive development.

item.title: Consciousness Takes Center Stage: IONS Sponsors The Science of Consciousness 2026 | IONS
item.description: IONS is proud to sponsor The Science of Consciousness 2026, the world’s longest-running consciousness conference.

item.title: Tribute to Ervin László – IONS
item.description: IONS Board Chair Claudia Welss remembers the extraordinary legacy of Ervin Laszlo.

item.title: Fourth Annual Noetic Sciences Research Prize to Explore UAP and Consciousness | IONS
item.description: The $100,000 Linda G. O’Bryant Prize will recognize bold new research frameworks investigating Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAP).

item.title: Rewiring Trauma: Why Veterans are Turning to Psychedelics and Holistic Healing for PTSD | IONS
item.description: Explore the science behind alternative PTSD relief for veterans, from psychedelic therapies like ibogaine and DMT to holistic methods.

item.title: What Happens When Three Channelers Describe the Same Extraterrestrials? | IONS
item.description: IONS researchers used a structured protocol to compare trance-channeled accounts attributed to extraterrestrial intelligences.

item.title: Is the Mind Quantum? – IONS
item.description: IONS Chief Scientist Dean Radin posits a new way to understand the mind-brain relationship and the implications of that idea.

item.title: Dean Radin on the Joe Rogan Experience – IONS
item.description: Dean Radin sat down with Joe Rogan for a milestone conversation about the science of consciousness.

item.title: Tech Futures | Montreal AI Ethics Institute
item.description: ✍️By Ismael Kherroubi Garcia, Founder & Co-lead of the Responsible Artificial Intelligence Network (RAIN), and Founder & CEO of Kairoi.

item.title: Celebrating the Legendary Life of Jean Houston – IONS
item.description: IONS honors the extraordinary life of Jean Houston.

item.title: The Twin Flame Journey: A Path to Noetic Wisdom | IONS
item.description: Theresa Cheung's latest book explores how the Twin Flame journey may be another language for noetic awakening and wisdom itself.

item.title: Keynote at MISSION Hubs Forum2026, Montreal: How does AI impact Creative Agency work? Avoiding Goodhart’s Law | Montreal AI Ethics Institute
item.description: ✍️ Column by Connor Wright, our Director of Partnerships. Photo Credit: Jackie Hutchinson on Unsplash Overview: Our Director of Partnerships, Connor Wright, was invited to speak at the MISSION Hubs…

item.title: Recess: Is AI in Law School a Helpful Tool or a Hidden Trap? | Montreal AI Ethics Institute
item.description: ✍️By Emma Edney from Encode Canada. Emma is a BCL/JD McCall MacBain Scholar candidate at McGill University. Her interests include ethical issues surrounding personal information in technology and the…

item.title: Recess: For students at one of Canada’s top universities, how much AI is too much AI when guidelines from professors remain unclear? | Montreal AI Ethics Institute
item.description: ✍️By Kennedy O’Neil from Encode Canada. Kennedy is an undergraduate sociology student at McGill University, with a minor in psychology, and a writer for Encode Canada. 📌 Editor’s Note: This piece is…

item.title: Tech Futures | Montreal AI Ethics Institute
item.description: ✍️By Ismael Kherroubi Garcia, Founder & Co-lead of the Responsible Artificial Intelligence Network (RAIN), and Founder & CEO of Kairoi.

item.title: How Americans are engaged with news, politics, religion and civic life
item.description: Numbers, Facts and Trends Shaping Your World

item.title: OII | The New Era of Lawyerless Contracting
item.description: Contract generators, platforms, and AI are reshaping consumer contracts. Luka Nenadic explains how and highlights the potential pitfalls and policy implications.

item.title: OII | How social media interoperability could restore user choice
item.description: Social networking interoperability is a leading policy proposal that gives users a real choice of platform providers. New evidence shows it only works if regulators get the specifics right, argues Philipp Riederle, doctoral researcher, OII.

item.title: OII | UK adults increasingly seek emotional support and companionship from AI, new report finds
item.description: New analysis from researchers at the Oxford Internet Institute reveals that users of artificial intelligence tools are increasingly turning to them for personal support, emotional advice, and companionship.

item.title: OII | AI-powered social media can subtly manipulate opinion at scale, new study finds
item.description: AI tools used to generate, edit or contextualise social media posts can introduce hidden biases that spread through online networks and shape public opinion, according to new research from the OII and the Hasso Plattner Institute.

item.title: OII | Keeping Europe’s Technological Choices Open
item.description: In an age of AI chokepoints, Europe’s choices will depend on the capacities it builds now. The OII's Johanna Ballesteros and TUM Think Tank's DR Nicklas Berild Lundblad explain why.

item.title: OII | Rethinking EU AI policy: why public subsidies for AI should deliver real wellbeing
item.description: A new working paper by OII researchers argues that EU technology policy should hold AI companies to account for actual public wellbeing, not just reduced risk.

item.title: OII | Expert Comment: Is banning under-16s from social media protection or exclusion?
item.description: The OII's Dr Vicki Nash warns that a social media ban risks leaving teenagers 'protected' but disempowered, cut off from news, learning and support at the very moment the UK is preparing to give 16-year-olds the vote.

item.title: OII | OII researchers head to FAccT 2026
item.description: Researchers and DPhil students from the Oxford Internet Institute are set to attend the Association of Computing Machinery (ACM) Conference on Fairness, Accountability and Transparency (FAccT) in Montréal, from 25-28 June 2026.

item.title: OII | The efficiency paradox in EU data centre policy
item.description: New EU reporting rules for data centre energy and water use may look like progress, but loopholes risk undermining genuine environmental accountability.

item.title: OII | Friendly AI chatbots make more mistakes and tell people what they want to hear, study finds
item.description: New Oxford research shows that training chatbots to sound warmer makes them up to 30% less accurate, and 40% more likely to validate users' false beliefs.

item.title: The Download: NASA’s new space telescope and OpenAI’s autonomous hacker
item.description: Plus: France has become the first EU country to ban social media for under-15s.

item.title: Shape-shifting mirrors on NASA’s new space telescope could unveil Jupiters like our own
item.description: The Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope will be the first space telescope to use an “active coronagraph” to block out unwanted starlight.

item.title: The Download: Chinese AI divides the White House, and a record copyright payout
item.description: Plus: Anthropic's record $1.5 billion copyright settlement has been approved.

item.title: Advancing next-gen AI with materials science innovation
item.description: As artificial intelligence pushes semiconductors and data centers to new physical limits, advances in materials science are becoming essential to sustaining the pace of innovation.

item.title: China’s AI models have Trump’s AI world at war with itself
item.description: Kimi and other free models from China have again been seen as a wake-up call. But for what?

item.title: The Download: AI hiring biases, and weather data sabotage
item.description: Plus: SpaceX is negotiating to sell the Pentagon AI compute.
